title: Executive / Senior Corporate Secretarial Executive
description:
• Provide a full spectrum of corporate secretarial services to a diverse portfolio of clients across various industries.
  • Ensure client entities comply with all statutory, regulatory, and governance requirements.
  • Draft, review, and coordinate the circulation of board minutes, directors’ resolutions (written), and shareholders’ resolutions.
  • Attend Board and committee meetings and prepare accurate and timely minutes.
  • Liaise and correspond effectively with clients, regulators, professional advisors, and other third parties.
  • Manage the incorporation of local and foreign companies, as well as the registration and deregistration of companies, Singapore branches, and Variable Capital Companies (VCCs), where applicable.
  • Prepare and lodge statutory filings, returns, and other regulatory submissions with ACRA and relevant authorities.
  • Maintain and update statutory records and registers in accordance with legal and regulatory requirements.
  • Ensure client entity records are kept accurate and up to date in ViewPoint and physical files through random checks and annual reviews, safeguarding the completeness and integrity of records within the assigned portfolio.
  • Conduct Know Your Client (KYC) screenings for new clients and ongoing monitoring of existing clients in line with the Company’s internal compliance policies, procedures, and controls.
  • Provide support and assistance to other members of the corporate services team as required.
  • Assist with work pass applications and related employment regulatory matters.
  • Handle regular payroll processing for selected clients.
  • Review payment requests and, where necessary, support urgent payments in the absence of the designated personnel.
  • Perform all other duties related to corporate secretarial services, including administrative support and ad hoc assignments as required.


Job Requirements

  • ICSA graduate or currently pursuing ICSA qualifications preferred; alternatively, a Degree or Diploma in Business Administration, Commerce, or a related discipline.
  • Minimum of 3–4 years of relevant experience, preferably gained within professional services firms.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications.
  • Strong command of English and Chinese, both written and spoken.
  • Ability to work independently with minimal supervision, as well as collaboratively within a team environment.
  • Well-versed in the requirements of the Companies Act and related regulations, with the ability to stay abreast of regulatory changes.

postedCompany.description:
ALPADIS (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD.

Alpadis Group is a Swiss-based company that provides multi-jurisdictional corporate and bespoke private client services, with offices in Switzerland, Singapore, Hong Kong, Labuan and Malaysia.

We are a team of individuals from all over the world and all walks of life, and we welcome talent no matter what shape or form. We offer a dynamic work environment that respects our employees’ right to self-development, learning, quality of life and health – while providing a pathway to a rewarding and fulfilling career.
postedCompany.name: ALPADIS (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
